_metadata.note: Written to repository root because the .geninsights/ directory cannot be auto-created by the agent runtime. Intended location is .geninsights/analysis/architecture_analysis.json.

analysis_metadata.architecture_style_detail: A single 50-line Python script executed by the Streamlit reactive script-runner model. No classes, no modules, no persistence layer, no external integrations at runtime.

layers.name: Presentation
layers.description: Browser-rendered Streamlit UI — HTML/CSS/JS delivered by Tornado and rendered by the browser's Streamlit React frontend. Includes page configuration, form widgets, and result display.
responsibilities: Render browser tab title, favicon, heading, caption
Collect two floating-point operands and arithmetic operation from user
Batch inputs via st.form to prevent partial-input recalculation
Render success result banner with equation string
Render expandable computation detail dictionary
Render error banner on division-by-zero

layers.name: Business Logic
layers.description: Arithmetic operation dispatch and business rule enforcement. Fully procedural if/elif/else branching. Embedded within app.py lines 24-39, immediately below the form.
responsibilities: Dispatch arithmetic computation based on selected operation string
Enforce BR-001: halt execution if num2 == 0 and operation == Divide
Compute result: num1 + num2, num1 - num2, num1 * num2, num1 / num2
Assign Unicode display symbol: +, −, ×, ÷

layers.name: Framework Infrastructure
layers.description: Streamlit framework internals that host and execute app.py. Not part of application source but essential architectural components.
responsibilities: Re-execute app.py from top-to-bottom on every browser interaction
Serve HTTP GET and WebSocket connections on localhost:8501
Persist widget values (num1, num2, operation, submitted) across script reruns
Transmit UI delta frames to browser

components.name: Page Configuration Block
responsibilities: Set browser tab title to 'Calculator'
Set page favicon to calculator emoji (🧮)
Set page layout to 'centered'
Render h1 heading 'Simple Calculator'
Render caption subtitle

components.name: Calculator Form Block
responsibilities: Render a Streamlit form named 'calculator_form'
Collect num1: float (first operand, default 0.0, 6 decimal places)
Collect num2: float (second operand, default 0.0, 6 decimal places)
Collect operation: str (one of Add, Subtract, Multiply, Divide; default Add)
Batch all inputs and gate calculation until Calculate button is pressed (BR-009)
Return submitted: bool flag

data_outputs.name: operation
data_outputs.description: Selected operation: Add | Subtract | Multiply | Divide

data_outputs.name: submitted
data_outputs.description: True when Calculate button was clicked in this script run

components.name: Calculation Engine
responsibilities: Execute only when submitted == True
Dispatch to arithmetic operation based on operation string (BR-008)
Compute result via Python built-in operators
Assign Unicode symbol for result display (BR-010)
Delegate to Division-by-Zero Guard before performing Divide

components.name: Division-by-Zero Guard
responsibilities: Check if num2 == 0 when operation is Divide
If violation: render st.error with user-friendly message
If violation: call st.stop() to immediately halt script execution
If no violation: allow execution to proceed to division computation
components.architectural_note: Uses Streamlit's st.stop() as an early-exit mechanism — analogous to a guard clause return in a function. Prevents any result from being computed or displayed on constraint violation.

components.name: Result Output Block
responsibilities: Render st.success banner with formatted equation string: '{num1} {symbol} {num2} = {result}'
Render st.expander labeled 'Computation details'
Inside expander: render st.write with detail dictionary (first_number, second_number, operation, result)

components.name: Streamlit Script Runner
responsibilities: Re-execute app.py from top to bottom on every user interaction
Inject widget values from Session State at the start of each rerun
Collect widget deltas during script execution
Send UI delta frames to the browser via Tornado

components.name: Tornado HTTP Server
responsibilities: Serve initial page load via HTTP GET on localhost:8501
Maintain WebSocket connections for live UI streaming
Route browser interactions to Script Runner

components.name: Session State Manager
responsibilities: Persist widget values (num1, num2, operation, submitted) between script reruns
Provide st.session_state dict (not explicitly used by app.py but implicit via widget keys)

patterns_detected.description: Streamlit re-executes the entire app.py script from top to bottom on every user interaction. Widget values from the previous run are restored from Session State. This is the core Streamlit execution model — the application code is stateless by design.
patterns_detected.benefit: No event handler boilerplate. Sequential code is easy to read and reason about.
patterns_detected.risk: Developers unfamiliar with Streamlit may accidentally create expensive side-effects that run on every rerun (e.g., database connections, file reads). Not an issue for this app but worth noting for future contributors.

patterns_detected.description: st.form wraps all input widgets so that no script rerun occurs until the user explicitly clicks the Calculate button. This prevents partial-input intermediate calculations.
patterns_detected.benefit: Prevents: (1) division-by-zero triggering while user is still typing, (2) premature results with default 0.0 operands.

patterns_detected.pattern: Guard Clause with Framework Early Exit
patterns_detected.description: Before performing division, a guard clause checks num2 == 0 and calls st.error() + st.stop(). st.stop() is a Streamlit-native mechanism that halts all further script execution immediately — analogous to a function's early return but at the framework level.
patterns_detected.benefit: Prevents division-by-zero with a clear user-facing error message. Clean separation: error path exits; success path continues.
patterns_detected.risk: Float equality comparison (num2 == 0) is technically fragile for IEEE 754 numbers. However, since Streamlit's number_input returns exactly 0.0 when the field is empty/zero, this is safe for this specific use case.

patterns_detected.description: A flat if/elif/else chain dispatches to the correct arithmetic operation based on the operation string value ('Add', 'Subtract', 'Multiply', 'Divide'). The final else clause implicitly handles 'Divide' (ISS-001 identified risk).
patterns_detected.benefit: Simple and readable for the current 4-operation set.
patterns_detected.risk: Implicit else is fragile. If the selectbox gains a 5th option, the new operation falls silently into the Divide branch. Should be made explicit: elif operation == 'Divide'.
patterns_detected.recommended_alternative: Dictionary dispatch: ops = {'Add': operator.add, ...}; result = ops[operation](num1, num2)

external_dependencies.name: Streamlit
external_dependencies.purpose: Provides all UI widgets, HTTP server, WebSocket protocol, session state, and script execution engine. The entire application UI and request lifecycle is managed by Streamlit.

external_dependencies.name: PyPI (Python Package Index)
external_dependencies.purpose: Hosts the streamlit package for installation. Not contacted at application runtime.

external_dependencies.name: Python Built-in Arithmetic
external_dependencies.purpose: All four arithmetic operations are performed using Python's built-in numeric operators. No math module or third-party math library is imported.

external_dependencies.name: Tornado
external_dependencies.purpose: Serves HTTP GET requests for initial page load and maintains WebSocket connections for live UI streaming. Not directly referenced in app.py.

diagrams.title: System Context (C4 Level 1)
diagrams.description: Shows the Simple Calculator system in its environment: End User, Web Browser, and PyPI (setup-time only). Demonstrates zero runtime external system dependencies.

diagrams.title: Component Architecture (C4 Level 2)
diagrams.description: Shows the three functional blocks of app.py (Page Config, Calculator Form, Calculation Engine+Output) alongside Streamlit framework internals (Script Runner, Tornado, Session State). Maps data flows between components.

diagrams.title: Technology Stack
diagrams.description: Full technology stack from browser (React frontend) through HTTP/WebSocket transport, Tornado server, Streamlit framework, Python interpreter, down to app.py source and virtual environment.

diagrams.title: Deployment Architecture
diagrams.description: Two-phase deployment diagram: Setup Phase (pip install from requirements.txt) and Runtime Phase (streamlit run app.py → Tornado :8501 → browser). Shows interaction flows between process and end user browser.

diagrams.title: Internal Dependencies
diagrams.description: Data flow dependencies between the three functional blocks and the Streamlit module. Shows variable passing (num1, num2, operation, submitted, result, symbol) between blocks.

metrics.primary_risk: Zero automated test coverage — no unit tests, no integration tests, no Streamlit AppTest usage

recommendations.title: Extract arithmetic logic into pure, testable functions
recommendations.description: Create a calculator.py module with a calculate(num1: float, op: str, num2: float) -> float function. This separates business logic from UI, enabling unit testing without rendering a Streamlit UI.

recommendations.title: Add unit tests using Streamlit AppTest
recommendations.description: Use Streamlit's built-in AppTest harness (from streamlit.testing.v1 import AppTest) to write end-to-end tests that simulate form submission and verify result rendering. This addresses the 62/100 health score's primary driver.

recommendations.title: Make Divide branch explicit (not implicit else)
recommendations.description: Replace the final `else:` branch with `elif operation == 'Divide':` to prevent future silent mismatch if the selectbox gains new operation options.

recommendations.title: Handle IEEE 754 overflow (inf / NaN) results
recommendations.description: After computing result, add: if not math.isfinite(result): st.error('Result is not a finite number.') and st.stop(). This covers very large float multiplication producing inf.

recommendations.title: Pin streamlit to an upper version bound
recommendations.description: Change requirements.txt from streamlit>=1.40.0 to streamlit>=1.40.0,<2.0.0 to prevent unexpected breaking-change upgrades in production or CI environments.
